subject: Cloud Computing
title: Redundancy and Backup Power
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Redundancy and Backup Power.

hook.text: When the power goes out at your house, your lights turn off. At a data center, nothing can stop.

buildup.text: Data centers use UPS batteries that kick in within milliseconds of a power failure.

discovery.text: Diesel generators start within seconds, providing power for days if the grid stays down.

twist.text: Everything is duplicated: power feeds, cooling systems, network connections. Failure of one changes nothing.

climax.text: Top-tier data centers guarantee 99.999% uptime — that's about five minutes of downtime per year.

punchline.text: The cloud stays on because everything exists twice. At least.

quiz.question: How do data centers handle power outages?
options: UPS batteries bridge the gap until diesel generators take over
They immediately shut down and restart when power returns
Solar panels on the roof provide emergency backup power
